lede: A look at August 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Fabrizio Donato and Steve Martin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2021 Haiti earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 7.2 earthquake strikes southwestern Haiti, killing at least 2,248 people and causing a humanitarian crisis.

events.title: Egypt
events.desc: Egypt declares a state of emergency as security forces kill hundreds of demonstrators supporting former president Mohamed Morsi.

events.title: Qahtaniyah bombings
events.desc: The Kahtaniya bombings kill at least 500 people.

events.title: Helios Airways Flight 522
events.desc: Helios Airways Flight 522, en route from Larnaca, Cyprus to Prague, Czech Republic via Athens, crashes in the hills near Grammatiko, Greece, killing 121 passengers and crew.

events.title: Lech Wałęsa
events.desc: Lech Wałęsa leads strikes at the Gdańsk, Poland shipyards.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Winston Churchill and Franklin D. Roosevelt sign the Atlantic Charter of war stating postwar aims.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Start of the Battle of Lorraine, an unsuccessful French offensive.

events.title: Tristan da Cunha
events.desc: The United Kingdom formally annexes the Tristan da Cunha archipelago, administering the islands from the Cape Colony in South Africa.

events.title: Villasur expedition
events.desc: The Spanish military Villasur expedition is defeated by Pawnee and Otoe warriors near present-day Columbus, Nebraska.

births.name: Steve Martin
births.line: American actor, comedian, musician, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Czesław Miłosz, Polish-born American novelist, essayist, and poet, Nobel Prize laureate
Bertolt Brecht, German poet, playwright, and director
Duncan I of Scotland, King of Alba from 1034 to 1040

observances.text: Christian feast day: Domingo Ibáñez de Erquicia
Christian feast day: Jonathan Myrick Daniels (Episcopal Church)
National Navajo Code Talkers Day is a holiday in the United States honoring Navajo code talkers in the military.
Falklands Day is the celebration of the first sighting of the Falkland Islands by John Davis in 1592.
Independence Day celebrates the independence of Pakistan from the United Kingdom in 1947.
